symbols.name: Water
common_meanings: The Holy Spirit in Christian tradition

biblical_references.context: Crossing the Red Sea - divine deliverance and new beginning
Jesus baptized in water - Holy Spirit presence
Still waters for comfort and restoration
River of life flowing from God's throne
Living water - eternal spiritual sustenance

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (chaos, unconscious) or The Self (wholeness)

symbols.name: Mountain

biblical_references.context: Mt. Sinai - God's presence and law given
The Sermon on the Mount - spiritual teaching
Lifting eyes to the mountains for help
Mt. Moriah - Abraham's test of faith
Transfiguration on the mountain - divine transformation

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (facing challenges) or The Wise Old Man/Woman (peak of wisdom)

symbols.name: Tree

biblical_references.context: Tree of Life in the Garden - eternal life
Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil
Righteous person planted by streams, bearing fruit
Good tree bears good fruit - you know people by their actions
Tree of Life in the New Jerusalem bearing twelve kinds of fruit

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Self (wholeness and integration) or The Wise Old Man/Woman (ancient wisdom)

symbols.name: Fire

biblical_references.context: Burning bush - God's presence in the fire
The voice of the Lord flashes with fire
The Lord will sit as a refiner with fire, purifying
Tongues of fire at Pentecost - Holy Spirit empowering
Refined by fire - spiritual refinement

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (destructive force) or The Self (transformative power)

symbols.name: Garden

biblical_references.context: The Garden of Eden - God's perfect provision
Garden as beloved, beauty and enclosed paradise
God makes waste places like the Garden of Eden
Gethsemane garden - place of submission to God's will
Jesus standing in a garden - resurrection and new life

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Mother (nourishment) or The Self (wholeness and harmony)

symbols.name: River

biblical_references.context: River gladdening the city of God
River flowing out of Eden to water the garden
Jordan River crossing - entering the promised land
River of the water of life flowing from God's throne
River flowing from temple bringing healing and life

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Journey (life's passage) or The Shadow (overwhelming power)

symbols.name: Storm

biblical_references.context: Jesus calms the storm - faith conquering fear
God speaks from the storm - divine wisdom
Storms and God's stilling power
Great storm sent by God - judgment and correction
Heavenly signs - storms as precursors to judgment

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (chaos) or The Self (testing)

symbols.name: Lion

biblical_references.context: The Lord is my light and salvation - the lion of my heart
Lion is mightiest among beasts
The devil prowls like a roaring lion seeking to devour
The Lion of the tribe of Judah - Jesus in power
Daniel in the lion's den - God's protection over the righteous

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (courage) or The Shadow (predatory force)

symbols.name: Eagle

biblical_references.context: I carried you on eagles' wings - God's protection
Under His wings you will find refuge
As an eagle stirs its nest, teaching young to fly
Living creatures - one like an eagle seeing all
Wherever the carcass is, the eagles gather - judgment

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Wise Old Man/Woman (distant perspective) or The Hero (soaring above)

symbols.name: Serpent

biblical_references.context: The serpent in the Garden - deception and fall
Be wise as serpents and innocent as doves
Bronze serpent - healing and deliverance
The dragon, that ancient serpent, called the devil
As Moses lifted up the serpent, so must the Son of Man be lifted up

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (temptation) or The Wise Old Man/Woman (hidden wisdom)

symbols.name: Lamb

biblical_references.context: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world
The Lamb who was slain is worthy of power and glory
Like a lamb led to slaughter - innocent suffering
The Lord is my shepherd - we are His sheep/lambs
Redeemed with the precious blood of Christ, like a lamb

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Child (innocence) or The Victim

symbols.name: Dove

biblical_references.context: Holy Spirit descending like a dove at Jesus' baptism
Dove returns with olive branch - peace after flood
Be innocent as doves
My dove is unique - beloved
Offering two young doves - redemption and purity

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Anima (feminine grace) or The Self (peace)

symbols.name: Fish

biblical_references.context: I will make you fishers of men
Jesus feeds 5,000 with five loaves and two fish
Parable of the net - separating good and bad fish
Miraculous catch of fish - abundance
Risen Jesus and the miraculous catch - provision and fellowship

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Self (life and provision) or The Child (feeding)

symbols.name: Wolf

biblical_references.context: False prophets come as wolves in sheep's clothing
The hired hand flees when the wolf comes
I am sending you as sheep in the midst of wolves
The wolf shall dwell with the lamb - restoration
Fierce wolves will come among you

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (predatory nature) or The Hero (fierce courage)

symbols.name: King
common_meanings: Jesus Christ, the King of Kings

biblical_references.context: The King of glory - God Almighty
King of kings and Lord of lords
Where is He who has been born King of the Jews
He has a name written on him - King of Kings
God will set up a kingdom that will never be destroyed

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (mastery) or The Wise Old Man/Woman (wisdom and rule)

symbols.name: Priest

biblical_references.context: Jesus, our great High Priest
You are a royal priesthood, a holy nation
Priests offering sacrifices before God
You are a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek
He made us a kingdom of priests

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Wise Old Man/Woman (spiritual wisdom) or The Caregiver

symbols.name: Stranger
common_meanings: Unknown or unfamiliar aspect of self

biblical_references.context: Whatever you did for the least of these strangers...
Do not forget to show hospitality to strangers, for some have entertained angels
Love the stranger as yourself
Abraham welcomes three strangers - angels unrecognized
We are strangers and sojourners on earth

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (unknown self) or The Messenger (divine messenger)

symbols.name: Child

biblical_references.context: Unless you become like a child, you won't enter the kingdom
Let the little children come to me
When I was a child, I spoke as a child; when I became a man, I put away childish things
Children are a heritage from the Lord
A little child will lead them

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Child (innocence and potential) or The Self (wholeness)

symbols.name: Angel

biblical_references.context: Gabriel appears to Mary - bearing divine message
He will command His angels to guard you
Angels are spirits sent to serve those who will inherit salvation
Angels came and attended to Jesus
Angels are stars in God's hand

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Messenger (divine communication) or The Wise Old Man/Woman

symbols.name: Door
common_meanings: Jesus as the door to salvation

biblical_references.context: I am the door; whoever enters through me will be saved
I stand at the door and knock; if anyone opens, I will come in
A great and effective door has opened for me
Go into your room, close the door, pray in secret
A door was opened to me in the Lord

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (what lies beyond) or Threshold Guardian

symbols.name: Key

biblical_references.context: I hold the keys of death and Hades
I give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven
You have taken away the key to knowledge
He who holds the key of David opens what no one can shut
Angel holding the key to the Abyss

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Wise Old Man/Woman (secret knowledge) or The Hero (mastery)

symbols.name: Crown

biblical_references.context: The crown of righteousness is laid up for me
Be faithful unto death, and I will give you the crown of life
An imperishable crown, we run the race
Crown of thorns placed on Jesus - mockery and redemption
Elders cast their crowns before the throne

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (achievement) or The Wise Old Man/Woman (authority)

symbols.name: Sword

biblical_references.context: The sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God
The word of God is sharper than any two-edged sword
I come not to bring peace, but a sword
A sharp sword coming from the mouth of Christ
As your sword has made women childless, so shall your mother be childless

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (warrior energy) or The Shadow (violence)

symbols.name: Bread

biblical_references.context: Jesus said, I am the bread of life
Give us today our daily bread
Jesus blessed and broke the bread and gave it to the disciples
We who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread
Come, eat my food and drink the wine I have mixed

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Mother (nourishment) or The Self (sustenance)

symbols.name: Wine

biblical_references.context: Drink this, it is my blood of the covenant
Jesus turns water into wine - abundance and blessing
Don't be filled with wine, but be filled with the Spirit
Wine gladdens the heart of man
The wine of God's wrath upon the earth

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Self (transformation) or The Shadow (intoxication)

symbols.name: Garment

biblical_references.context: He has clothed me with garments of salvation
Wedding garment parable - wearing the right attire
She is given fine linen, bright and clean - righteousness
Put on compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience
Bring the best robe and put it on him - the prodigal son

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Anima/Animus (persona) or The Self (identity)

symbols.name: Vessel
common_meanings: The body as a vessel for the spirit

biblical_references.context: We have this treasure in jars of clay - power in weakness
Each should know how to possess his vessel in holiness
In a household are vessels of gold and vessels of wood - noble use
Ananias, he is a chosen instrument of mine
The potter has the right to make vessels for honorable and dishonorable use

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (emptiness) or The Self (container of wholeness)

symbols.name: Flying
common_meanings: Out of control (falling from the sky)

biblical_references.context: Under His wings you will find refuge
Those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength, mount up with wings like eagles
Wheels and wings moving in perfect harmony - God's throne vision
Living creatures with wings - celestial worship
Elijah taken up to heaven in a whirlwind

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (transcendence) or The Self (spiritual elevation)

symbols.name: Falling
common_meanings: Spiritual decline or separation from God

biblical_references.context: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall
They will lift you up in their hands, so you will not strike your foot against a stone
All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
Stars falling from the heavens - cosmic upheaval
I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (descent into chaos) or The Child (vulnerability)

symbols.name: Running

biblical_references.context: Let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us
Run in such a way as to get the prize
The sun runs its course with joy like a strong man
They will run and not grow weary
Rebekah ran to meet the servant - urgent meeting

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (pursuing goals) or The Shadow (fleeing danger)

symbols.name: Climbing

biblical_references.context: Who may ascend the mountain of the Lord? Those with clean hands and pure hearts
Jesus took Peter, James and John, and led them up a high mountain
The mountain of the Lord will be established as the highest of mountains
You will see heaven open, and angels ascending and descending
Jacob's ladder with angels ascending and descending

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (striving) or The Shadow (climbing for selfish gain)

symbols.name: Crossing Water
common_meanings: New beginning on the other side

biblical_references.context: Crossing the Jordan River into the promised land
Crossing the Red Sea with walls of water
Jesus calms the storm while crossing the sea
No more death or suffering - a new beginning
Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Journey (transformation) or The Shadow (danger)

symbols.name: Building

biblical_references.context: We are co-workers in God's service; you are God's building
On this rock I will build my church
In him the whole building is joined together and rises to become a holy temple
Tower of Babel - human ambition vs. God's will
Let us rebuild the wall of Jerusalem

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Hero (creation) or The Self (wholeness through construction)

symbols.name: Temple

biblical_references.context: Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit?
When Christ came as high priest of the good things that are now already here
Jesus said, destroy this temple, and I will raise it again in three days
I did not see a temple in the city, because the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple
One thing I ask of the Lord, this is what I seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Self (sacred center) or The Wise Old Man/Woman

symbols.name: City

biblical_references.context: The Holy City, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven
For here we do not have an enduring city, but we are looking for the city that is to come
Come, let us build ourselves a city - human ambition
Great is the Lord and most worthy of praise, in the city of our God
The wealth of the rich is their fortified city

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Self (interconnectedness) or The Shadow (worldly chaos)

symbols.name: Wilderness

biblical_references.context: Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted
40 years in the wilderness - testing and provision
In a dry and weary land where there is no water
Jesus went to a solitary place to pray
The woman fled into the wilderness to be taken care of

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (the unknown) or The Hero (testing)

symbols.name: Valley

biblical_references.context: Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil
The valley of Jehoshaphat - judgment
The water flowed down into the valleys
Every valley shall be raised up, every mountain made low
I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys

symbols.jungian_archetype: The Shadow (descent) or The Mother (shelter)
